body_txt: A character who does not appear for much of or possibly all of the plot, but whose presence is nevertheless felt. More accurately, the absence of the character is most significant. These works show what effect their absence has on the world and the characters. The character, in their absence, pulls strings or drives action, becoming a sort of MacGuffin or shadowy influence. In short, the character drives the plot despite their absence either directly or through the minds and hearts of the characters. This is usually done in a few ways: Compare The Ghost and King in the Mountain . A Posthumous Character is influential despite being dead and the Plot-Triggering Death and Cynicism Catalyst because of it, and The Lost Lenore is for dead love interests. The opposite of this is Chuck Cunningham Syndrome and Forgotten Fallen Friend . For characters with a more central role and presence, see Removing the Crucial Teammate .
